untranslatable_reason: Signal requires identification of futures contract expiration day (a calendar/contract-specific event) and GARCH variance equation coefficients (0.819), which are not available in the standard technical indicator set. The backtest engine lacks: (1) expiration_day dummy variable, (2) GARCH volatility model parameters. Cannot construct precise entry conditions without these structural/statistical indicators.
translation_notes: The signal is fundamentally a volatility regime detection rule tied to specific calendar events (futures expiration) and estimated GARCH model coefficients from econometric analysis. This requires either: custom calendar logic for contract expiration dates, or pre-calculated GARCH variance features. Placeholder condition uses india_vix as a volatility proxy, but does not capture the expiration-day-specific effect or GARCH coefficient threshold (0.819). Hold period set to 1 day (expiration day + day after). Direction is BOTH because signal expects volatility increases regardless of price direction.
